framework_name: NIS2 Directive - Network and Information Security
disclaimer: This report assists with NIS2 Directive technical compliance readiness assessment for AWS infrastructure. It does not constitute formal NIS2 compliance certification or replace assessment by a national competent authority. NIS2 compliance requires organizational, governance, and incident reporting measures beyond technical controls. Member State transposition laws may impose additional requirements.

NIS2-RM-01.title: Risk analysis and information system security policies
NIS2-RM-01.section: Article 21(2)(a) - Risk Analysis and IS Security Policies
NIS2-RM-01.description: Essential and important entities shall adopt policies on risk analysis and information system security, including risk identification, assessment, and treatment aligned with the entity's risk appetite and the state of the art.
NIS2-RM-01.manual_steps: Automated checks verify that threat detection services (GuardDuty, Security Hub) and IAM Access Analyzer are enabled and findings are addressed. Manual review required: documented risk analysis policy, risk appetite statement, information security policy approved by management body, and evidence of regular policy review.
NIS2-RM-01.evidence_template: Automated risk identification services verified: GuardDuty enabled and findings addressed, Security Hub enabled, IAM Access Analyzer active. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary} Manual review of risk analysis and IS security policies is also required per Article 21(2)(a).

NIS2-RM-01a.title: Information security policy framework
NIS2-RM-01a.section: Article 21(2)(a) - Risk Analysis and IS Security Policies
NIS2-RM-01a.description: The entity shall maintain an information security policy framework that is approved by the management body, communicated to all relevant stakeholders, and reviewed at planned intervals or when significant changes occur.
NIS2-RM-01a.manual_steps: Review the information security policy document, management body approval records, communication evidence, distribution logs, and review schedule. Verify policies cover all areas required by Article 21(2)(a)-(j).
NIS2-RM-01a.evidence_template: NIS2-RM-01a requires manual assessment. Review information security policy framework, management body approval, and review cycle documentation.

NIS2-RM-01b.title: Asset inventory and classification
NIS2-RM-01b.section: Article 21(2)(a) - Risk Analysis and IS Security Policies
NIS2-RM-01b.description: The entity shall maintain an inventory of network and information system assets relevant to the security of network and information systems, and classify them according to their criticality.
NIS2-RM-01b.manual_steps: Automated checks verify that AWS Config is enabled and recording resource inventory, stopped instances are identified as potentially orphaned assets, and unused Elastic IPs are flagged. Manual review required: formal asset register, classification scheme, data flow mapping, and system boundary documentation.
NIS2-RM-01b.evidence_template: Asset discovery infrastructure verified: AWS Config recorder enabled and active, stopped EC2 instances identified, unused Elastic IPs flagged. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary} Manual review of formal asset inventory and classification scheme is also required.

NIS2-RM-01c.title: Risk assessment methodology
NIS2-RM-01c.section: Article 21(2)(a) - Risk Analysis and IS Security Policies
NIS2-RM-01c.description: The entity shall implement a systematic risk assessment methodology that identifies threats and vulnerabilities to its network and information systems, assesses likelihood and impact, and determines appropriate risk treatment measures.
NIS2-RM-01c.manual_steps: Review the documented risk assessment methodology, risk register with identified threats and vulnerabilities, risk treatment plans, residual risk acceptance records, and evidence of periodic reassessment.
NIS2-RM-01c.evidence_template: NIS2-RM-01c requires manual assessment. Review risk assessment methodology, risk register, and risk treatment plans.

NIS2-RM-02.title: Incident handling
NIS2-RM-02.description: Essential and important entities shall implement incident handling procedures covering prevention, detection, analysis, containment, response, and recovery from incidents affecting the security of network and information systems.
NIS2-RM-02.manual_steps: Automated checks verify that threat detection (GuardDuty, Security Hub), critical event alarms (root usage, unauthorized API calls, authentication failures), and security contact registration are in place. Manual review required: documented incident handling procedures, incident classification taxonomy, escalation matrix, and communication plan.
NIS2-RM-02.evidence_template: Incident detection infrastructure verified: GuardDuty enabled and findings addressed, Security Hub enabled, CloudWatch alarms for root usage and unauthorized API calls and authentication failures configured, security contact registered. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary} Manual review of incident handling procedures is also required per Article 21(2)(b).

NIS2-RM-02a.title: Incident detection capability
NIS2-RM-02a.description: The entity shall implement technical measures to detect incidents affecting the security of network and information systems, including monitoring of security events, anomaly detection, and threat intelligence integration.
NIS2-RM-02a.manual_steps: All checks are automated. Verify: GuardDuty enabled with findings addressed, Security Hub enabled, CloudTrail enabled with log validation, CloudTrail delivers to CloudWatch Logs, CloudWatch log group KMS encryption, VPC flow logs active, and all 15 recommended CloudWatch alarms configured for critical security events (root usage, unauthorized API calls, console sign-in without MFA, IAM policy changes, CloudTrail changes, authentication failures, CMK deletion, S3 policy changes, Config changes, security group changes, NACL changes, gateway changes, route table changes, VPC changes, Organizations changes).
NIS2-RM-02a.evidence_template: Incident detection capability verified across threat detection services and security event monitoring: GuardDuty, Security Hub, CloudTrail with log validation, CloudTrail CloudWatch Logs delivery, CloudWatch log group KMS encryption, VPC flow logs, and 15 CloudWatch security alarms. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

NIS2-RM-02b.title: Incident response plan
NIS2-RM-02b.description: The entity shall maintain an incident response plan that defines roles and responsibilities, classification criteria, escalation procedures, communication protocols, and lessons-learned processes. The plan shall be tested regularly.
NIS2-RM-02b.manual_steps: Automated checks verify that an AWS support role exists for incident escalation and that a security contact is registered. Manual review required: documented incident response plan, defined roles and responsibilities, escalation matrix, communication templates, evidence of tabletop exercises or incident simulations, and lessons-learned process.
NIS2-RM-02b.evidence_template: Incident response readiness verified: AWS support role exists for escalation, security contact registered for notifications. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary} Manual review of incident response plan and testing evidence is also required.

NIS2-RM-02c.title: Incident notification (Article 23 timelines)
NIS2-RM-02c.description: The entity shall have processes in place to meet NIS2 incident notification timelines: 24-hour early warning to the CSIRT or competent authority, 72-hour incident notification with initial assessment, and a final report within one month. Significant incidents are those causing severe operational disruption, financial loss, or affecting other persons.
NIS2-RM-02c.manual_steps: Review incident notification procedures, CSIRT contact details, notification templates, and evidence of process testing. Verify the entity has identified its national CSIRT and competent authority under Member State transposition law. Confirm processes cover: (1) 24h early warning with indication of suspected unlawful or malicious cause, (2) 72h notification with initial assessment including severity and impact, (3) final report within one month with root cause and mitigation measures.
NIS2-RM-02c.evidence_template: NIS2-RM-02c requires manual assessment. Review incident notification procedures, CSIRT contact information, notification templates, and evidence of compliance with Article 23 timelines (24h early warning, 72h notification, 1-month final report).

NIS2-RM-03.title: Business continuity and crisis management
NIS2-RM-03.section: Article 21(2)(c) - Business Continuity and Crisis Management
NIS2-RM-03.description: Essential and important entities shall implement business continuity measures including backup management, disaster recovery, and crisis management to ensure the continuity of services provided by their network and information systems.
NIS2-RM-03.manual_steps: Automated checks verify that S3 versioning (for data recovery) and RDS Multi-AZ (for database availability) are enabled. Manual review required: business continuity plan, business impact analysis, recovery time objectives (RTO) and recovery point objectives (RPO), crisis management procedures, and evidence of BCP testing.
NIS2-RM-03.evidence_template: Business continuity technical controls verified: S3 versioning for data recoverability, RDS Multi-AZ for database high availability. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary} Manual review of business continuity plan and crisis management procedures is also required per Article 21(2)(c).

NIS2-RM-03a.title: Backup management
NIS2-RM-03a.section: Article 21(2)(c) - Business Continuity and Crisis Management
NIS2-RM-03a.description: The entity shall implement backup policies and procedures that ensure data and system configurations can be restored in the event of an incident. Backups shall be tested regularly and stored securely.
NIS2-RM-03a.manual_steps: Verify: S3 versioning enabled for data recovery, MFA Delete on critical S3 buckets to prevent backup destruction, RDS Multi-AZ for automated failover, encryption at rest for backup data integrity (S3, RDS, EBS), S3 lifecycle policies for data retention management, AWS Backup vault with backup plan for centralized backup management. Review backup schedules, retention periods, and evidence of backup restoration testing.
NIS2-RM-03a.evidence_template: Backup management controls verified: S3 versioning enabled, MFA Delete protection on S3 buckets, RDS Multi-AZ enabled, encryption at rest for S3 buckets, RDS instances, and EBS volumes, S3 lifecycle policies configured, AWS Backup vault with backup plan. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

NIS2-RM-03b.title: Disaster recovery
NIS2-RM-03b.section: Article 21(2)(c) - Business Continuity and Crisis Management
NIS2-RM-03b.description: The entity shall maintain disaster recovery procedures that enable the restoration of network and information systems to a defined level of service within agreed timeframes following an incident.
NIS2-RM-03b.manual_steps: Automated checks verify RDS Multi-AZ for automated failover and EC2 termination protection for critical instances. Manual review required: documented disaster recovery plan, defined RTO and RPO targets, cross-region or cross-account recovery capability, evidence of DR testing, and failover procedures.
NIS2-RM-03b.evidence_template: Disaster recovery technical controls verified: RDS Multi-AZ for automated failover, EC2 termination protection for critical instances. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary} Manual review of disaster recovery plan and testing evidence is also required.

NIS2-RM-03c.title: Crisis management procedures
NIS2-RM-03c.section: Article 21(2)(c) - Business Continuity and Crisis Management
NIS2-RM-03c.description: The entity shall establish crisis management procedures that define decision-making authority, communication channels, and escalation paths to be used during a crisis affecting the security of network and information systems.
NIS2-RM-03c.manual_steps: Review crisis management plan, decision-making authority matrix, internal and external communication procedures, crisis team composition and contact details, and evidence of crisis simulation exercises.
NIS2-RM-03c.evidence_template: NIS2-RM-03c requires manual assessment. Review crisis management procedures, decision-making authority matrix, communication plans, and crisis exercise records.

NIS2-RM-04.title: Supply chain security
NIS2-RM-04.description: Essential and important entities shall address security in the supply chain, including security-related aspects concerning the relationships between each entity and its direct suppliers or service providers. Entities shall take into account the vulnerabilities specific to each direct supplier and service provider, the overall quality of products and cybersecurity practices of suppliers, and the results of coordinated security risk assessments.
NIS2-RM-04.manual_steps: Review supplier and service provider register, supply chain risk assessment methodology, contractual security requirements for suppliers, evidence of supplier due diligence, and processes for monitoring supplier security posture. NIS2 Article 22 enables coordinated risk assessments of critical supply chains at EU level.
NIS2-RM-04.evidence_template: NIS2-RM-04 requires manual assessment. Review supply chain security policies, supplier risk assessments, contractual security requirements, and supplier monitoring processes per Article 21(2)(d).

NIS2-RM-04a.title: Supplier risk assessment
NIS2-RM-04a.description: The entity shall assess risks associated with direct suppliers and service providers, considering the criticality of the supplied product or service, the supplier's cybersecurity practices, and the potential impact of a supply chain compromise on the entity's network and information systems.
NIS2-RM-04a.manual_steps: Review supplier risk assessment records, supplier classification by criticality, assessment criteria (including cybersecurity maturity of suppliers), and evidence of periodic reassessment. Consider results of any EU-level coordinated risk assessments under Article 22.
NIS2-RM-04a.evidence_template: NIS2-RM-04a requires manual assessment. Review supplier risk assessment records and supplier criticality classification.

NIS2-RM-04b.title: Secure development practices
NIS2-RM-04b.description: The entity shall ensure that security is integrated into the development lifecycle of products and services, including secure coding practices, code review, and security testing.
NIS2-RM-04b.manual_steps: Automated checks verify that Lambda functions use supported runtimes (reducing vulnerability to known runtime flaws) and that ECS task definitions do not grant excessive privileges. Manual review required: secure development lifecycle (SDLC) documentation, code review processes, static and dynamic analysis tooling, and security testing procedures.
NIS2-RM-04b.evidence_template: Secure development controls verified: Lambda functions using supported runtimes, ECS task definitions reviewed for privilege restrictions. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary} Manual review of SDLC documentation and security testing procedures is also required.

NIS2-RM-04c.title: Vulnerability handling in supply chain
NIS2-RM-04c.description: The entity shall implement processes for identifying, tracking, and remediating vulnerabilities in products and services obtained from suppliers, including coordinated vulnerability disclosure.
NIS2-RM-04c.manual_steps: Automated checks verify RDS auto minor version upgrade for database engine patches, Lambda runtime currency, and SSM managed instances with compliance status. Manual review required: vulnerability management policy, coordinated vulnerability disclosure process, patch tracking for third-party components, and evidence of vulnerability remediation timelines.
NIS2-RM-04c.evidence_template: Supply chain vulnerability handling controls verified: RDS auto minor version upgrade enabled, Lambda runtime currency checked, SSM managed instances and compliance status reviewed. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary} Manual review of vulnerability management and coordinated disclosure processes is also required.

NIS2-RM-05.title: Security in network and information systems acquisition, development and maintenance
NIS2-RM-05.section: Article 21(2)(e) - Secure Acquisition, Development and Maintenance
NIS2-RM-05.description: Essential and important entities shall implement security in the acquisition, development and maintenance of network and information systems, including vulnerability handling and disclosure.
NIS2-RM-05.manual_steps: Automated checks verify automated patching capabilities (RDS auto upgrade, SSM managed instances, SSM compliance), runtime currency (Lambda), and configuration tracking (AWS Config). Manual review required: security requirements in procurement processes, secure development standards, change management procedures, and vulnerability disclosure policy.
NIS2-RM-05.evidence_template: Secure acquisition and maintenance controls verified: RDS auto minor version upgrade, Lambda runtime currency, SSM managed instances and compliance, AWS Config recording. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary} Manual review of procurement security requirements and development standards is also required per Article 21(2)(e).

NIS2-RM-05a.title: Vulnerability handling and disclosure
NIS2-RM-05a.section: Article 21(2)(e) - Secure Acquisition, Development and Maintenance
NIS2-RM-05a.description: The entity shall implement processes for identifying, assessing, and remediating vulnerabilities in its network and information systems. The entity shall participate in coordinated vulnerability disclosure as encouraged by Article 12.
NIS2-RM-05a.manual_steps: Automated checks verify vulnerability identification services (GuardDuty findings, Security Hub findings, IAM Access Analyzer findings, Amazon Inspector) and patch management (SSM managed instances, SSM compliance). Manual review required: vulnerability disclosure policy, vulnerability scanning schedule, remediation SLAs, and participation in coordinated disclosure.
NIS2-RM-05a.evidence_template: Vulnerability handling controls verified: GuardDuty enabled and findings addressed, Security Hub enabled, IAM Access Analyzer active, Amazon Inspector enabled for vulnerability scanning, SSM managed instances and compliance status checked. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary} Manual review of vulnerability disclosure policy and remediation SLAs is also required.

NIS2-RM-05b.title: Secure configuration management
NIS2-RM-05b.section: Article 21(2)(e) - Secure Acquisition, Development and Maintenance
NIS2-RM-05b.description: The entity shall establish and maintain secure configurations for network and information systems, ensuring that default configurations are hardened and that configuration changes are tracked and authorized.
NIS2-RM-05b.manual_steps: Verify: AWS Config enabled and recording configuration changes, default VPC usage reviewed, default security group rules locked down, VPC subnet isolation (public/private separation) verified, EC2 instances use IMDSv2 (hardened metadata), default EBS encryption enabled, IAM password policy meets organizational standards, ECS container health checks configured, ECS logging enabled, Lambda functions not using deprecated runtimes, WAFv2 WebACL exists for web application protection. Review configuration management policy and change authorization procedures.
NIS2-RM-05b.evidence_template: Secure configuration management verified: AWS Config enabled and recording, default VPC usage reviewed, default security group hardened, VPC subnet isolation verified, IMDSv2 enforced, default EBS encryption enabled, IAM password policy configured, ECS health checks and logging in place, Lambda runtime configuration reviewed, WAFv2 WebACL verified. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

NIS2-RM-05c.title: Patch management
NIS2-RM-05c.section: Article 21(2)(e) - Secure Acquisition, Development and Maintenance
NIS2-RM-05c.description: The entity shall implement a patch management process that ensures timely application of security patches to network and information systems, prioritized according to the severity of vulnerabilities.
NIS2-RM-05c.manual_steps: Verify: RDS auto minor version upgrade for database patches, Lambda functions using supported runtimes, SSM-managed instances for centralized patching, SSM compliance status for patch verification, EC2 patch compliance via SSM. Review patch management policy, patch testing procedures, and emergency patching process.
NIS2-RM-05c.evidence_template: Patch management controls verified: RDS auto minor version upgrade enabled, Lambda runtime currency checked, SSM managed instances verified, SSM compliance status reviewed, EC2 patch compliance via SSM verified. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

NIS2-RM-06.title: Policies and procedures to assess the effectiveness of cybersecurity risk-management measures
NIS2-RM-06.description: Essential and important entities shall adopt policies and procedures to assess the effectiveness of their cybersecurity risk-management measures, including security testing, auditing, and continuous monitoring.
NIS2-RM-06.manual_steps: Automated checks verify that continuous monitoring services (GuardDuty, Security Hub, AWS Config, CloudTrail) are enabled to provide ongoing assessment of control effectiveness. Manual review required: security audit program, penetration testing schedule, control effectiveness metrics, management review process, and evidence of corrective actions from assessments.
NIS2-RM-06.evidence_template: Continuous monitoring for effectiveness assessment verified: GuardDuty enabled, Security Hub enabled, AWS Config enabled and recording, CloudTrail enabled. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary} Manual review of security audit program and effectiveness assessment procedures is also required per Article 21(2)(f).

NIS2-RM-06a.title: Security testing and auditing
NIS2-RM-06a.description: The entity shall conduct regular security testing (including penetration testing and vulnerability assessments) and auditing of its network and information systems to verify that cybersecurity measures operate effectively.
NIS2-RM-06a.manual_steps: Review security testing program documentation, penetration test reports, vulnerability assessment results, audit findings, remediation tracking, and evidence that testing covers all critical systems within the NIS2 scope. Essential entities may be subject to targeted security audits by competent authorities under Article 32.
NIS2-RM-06a.evidence_template: NIS2-RM-06a requires manual assessment. Review penetration testing reports, vulnerability assessment results, audit findings, and remediation tracking.

NIS2-RM-06b.title: Compliance monitoring
NIS2-RM-06b.description: The entity shall implement continuous compliance monitoring to detect deviations from established security policies and standards, and to verify that risk-management measures remain effective over time.
NIS2-RM-06b.manual_steps: Automated checks verify that compliance monitoring infrastructure is in place: AWS Config for configuration compliance, Security Hub for security standards, CloudTrail for audit trail integrity. Manual review required: compliance monitoring schedule, deviation handling procedures, and reporting to management body.
NIS2-RM-06b.evidence_template: Compliance monitoring infrastructure verified: AWS Config enabled and recording, Security Hub enabled, CloudTrail enabled with log validation. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary} Manual review of compliance monitoring procedures and deviation handling is also required.

NIS2-RM-07.title: Basic cyber hygiene practices and cybersecurity training
NIS2-RM-07.description: Essential and important entities shall adopt basic cyber hygiene practices and provide cybersecurity training for staff. The management body shall undergo specific training to gain sufficient knowledge and skills to identify risks and assess cybersecurity practices (Article 20(2)).
NIS2-RM-07.manual_steps: Automated checks verify basic cyber hygiene technical controls: root MFA, user MFA, password policy, key rotation, and unused key cleanup. Manual review required: cybersecurity awareness training program, training records, management body training (per Article 20(2)), phishing simulation results, and acceptable use policies.
NIS2-RM-07.evidence_template: Cyber hygiene technical controls verified: root account MFA, user MFA enforcement, IAM password policy, access key rotation within 90 days, unused keys identified. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary} Manual review of cybersecurity training program and management body training is also required per Article 21(2)(g) and Article 20(2).

NIS2-RM-07a.title: Security awareness training
NIS2-RM-07a.description: The entity shall provide regular security awareness training to all staff, covering topics such as phishing, social engineering, password security, and incident reporting. Article 20(2) specifically requires that members of the management body follow training and offer similar training to employees on a regular basis.
NIS2-RM-07a.manual_steps: Review security awareness training program content, delivery schedule, attendance records, training effectiveness metrics (e.g., phishing simulation results), and evidence that management body members have completed training per Article 20(2).
NIS2-RM-07a.evidence_template: NIS2-RM-07a requires manual assessment. Review security awareness training program, attendance records, management body training evidence, and training effectiveness metrics.

NIS2-RM-07b.title: Authentication and access policies
NIS2-RM-07b.description: The entity shall implement authentication and access policies as part of basic cyber hygiene, including strong password requirements, credential rotation, and removal of unnecessary access.
NIS2-RM-07b.manual_steps: Verify: IAM password policy enforces complexity and rotation, access keys are rotated within 90 days, unused access keys are identified and removed, users do not have multiple active access keys, expired SSL/TLS certificates are removed. Review authentication and access policies documentation.
NIS2-RM-07b.evidence_template: Authentication and access hygiene verified: IAM password policy configured, access keys rotated within 90 days, unused keys flagged, multiple active keys identified, expired certificates detected. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

NIS2-RM-08.title: Policies and procedures regarding the use of cryptography and, where appropriate, encryption
NIS2-RM-08.description: Essential and important entities shall adopt policies and procedures regarding the use of cryptography and, where appropriate, encryption, to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and authenticity of data processed by their network and information systems.
NIS2-RM-08.manual_steps: Verify: encryption at rest for S3, EBS, RDS, and EFS; encryption in transit for S3 (HTTPS enforced); CloudTrail log encryption with KMS; KMS key rotation and key policies. Review cryptography policy, approved algorithms and key lengths, and encryption requirements per data classification.
NIS2-RM-08.evidence_template: Cryptography and encryption controls verified: S3 encryption at rest, EBS encryption, default EBS encryption, RDS encryption, EFS encryption, S3 HTTPS enforcement, CloudTrail KMS encryption, KMS key rotation, KMS key policies reviewed. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

NIS2-RM-08a.title: Encryption at rest
NIS2-RM-08a.description: The entity shall ensure that data stored in network and information systems is encrypted at rest using approved cryptographic algorithms, commensurate with the data classification level.
NIS2-RM-08a.manual_steps: Verify: S3 bucket encryption enabled, EBS volumes encrypted, default EBS encryption enabled for the account, RDS instances encrypted, EFS file systems encrypted, CloudTrail logs encrypted with KMS. Review data classification scheme and encryption requirements per classification.
NIS2-RM-08a.evidence_template: Encryption at rest verified: S3 bucket encryption, EBS volume encryption, default EBS encryption, RDS instance encryption, EFS file system encryption, CloudTrail KMS encryption. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

NIS2-RM-08b.title: Encryption in transit
NIS2-RM-08b.description: The entity shall ensure that data in transit is protected using approved cryptographic protocols, including enforcement of TLS for API and service communications.
NIS2-RM-08b.manual_steps: Automated checks verify that S3 buckets enforce HTTPS-only access and that expired SSL/TLS certificates are identified. Manual review required: TLS policy for all services, minimum TLS version requirements, certificate management procedures, and network encryption between components.
NIS2-RM-08b.evidence_template: Encryption in transit controls verified: S3 HTTPS enforcement (deny HTTP), expired SSL/TLS certificates identified. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary} Manual review of TLS policies and certificate management is also required.

NIS2-RM-08c.title: Key management
NIS2-RM-08c.description: The entity shall implement cryptographic key management procedures covering key generation, distribution, storage, rotation, revocation, and destruction.
NIS2-RM-08c.manual_steps: Verify: KMS customer-managed keys have automatic rotation enabled, KMS key policies restrict access appropriately, Secrets Manager secrets have rotation configured, Secrets Manager secrets are actually being rotated. Review key management policy and key lifecycle procedures.
NIS2-RM-08c.evidence_template: Key management controls verified: KMS key rotation enabled, KMS key policies reviewed, Secrets Manager rotation configured, Secrets Manager rotation status verified. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

NIS2-RM-09.title: Human resources security, access control policies and asset management
NIS2-RM-09.section: Article 21(2)(i) - HR Security, Access Control and Asset Management
NIS2-RM-09.description: Essential and important entities shall adopt human resources security measures, access control policies, and asset management practices to ensure that access to network and information systems is appropriately controlled throughout the employment lifecycle.
NIS2-RM-09.manual_steps: Automated checks verify that administrative privileges are restricted (no wildcard policies), permissions are managed through groups (not direct user attachment), IAM Access Analyzer identifies unintended access, unused keys are flagged, and root access keys are disabled. Manual review required: HR security policy (pre-employment screening, onboarding, offboarding), access control policy, and asset management procedures.
NIS2-RM-09.evidence_template: Access control and HR security controls verified: no wildcard admin policies, group-based permission management, IAM Access Analyzer enabled, unused access keys flagged, root access keys disabled. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary} Manual review of HR security, access control, and asset management policies is also required per Article 21(2)(i).

NIS2-RM-09a.title: Identity and access management
NIS2-RM-09a.section: Article 21(2)(i) - HR Security, Access Control and Asset Management
NIS2-RM-09a.description: The entity shall implement identity and access management controls that enforce the principle of least privilege, ensure proper authentication, and provide visibility into access patterns.
NIS2-RM-09a.manual_steps: Verify: no wildcard admin policies, policies assigned through groups not directly to users, IAM Access Analyzer active, OIDC trust policies include sub condition, EC2 instances use IAM roles, security groups restrict open access, NACLs are not unrestricted, default security group rules locked down, no public AMIs exposing internal images, no public S3 buckets, no public RDS instances, Lambda resource policies reviewed. Review IAM strategy documentation and access request procedures.
NIS2-RM-09a.evidence_template: Identity and access management verified: no wildcard admin policies, group-based permissions, IAM Access Analyzer enabled, OIDC trust conditions verified, EC2 instance roles used, security groups reviewed, NACLs reviewed, default security group hardened, no public AMIs, no public S3 buckets, no public RDS instances, Lambda resource policies reviewed. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

NIS2-RM-09b.title: Privileged access management
NIS2-RM-09b.section: Article 21(2)(i) - HR Security, Access Control and Asset Management
NIS2-RM-09b.description: The entity shall implement controls for privileged access management, including protection of root and administrative accounts, monitoring of privileged operations, and restriction of privilege escalation paths.
NIS2-RM-09b.manual_steps: Verify: root account MFA enabled, no root access keys, hardware MFA for root, no wildcard admin policies, CloudShell access reviewed, IAM role session duration limits checked, CloudWatch alarm for root account usage, alarm for IAM policy changes. Review privileged access management procedures, just-in-time access provisioning, and privileged session monitoring.
NIS2-RM-09b.evidence_template: Privileged access management verified: root MFA, no root access keys, hardware MFA for root, admin policy restrictions, CloudShell access reviewed, IAM role session duration limits, root usage alarm, IAM policy change alarm. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

NIS2-RM-09c.title: Access review and deprovisioning
NIS2-RM-09c.section: Article 21(2)(i) - HR Security, Access Control and Asset Management
NIS2-RM-09c.description: The entity shall conduct regular access reviews and ensure timely deprovisioning of access when no longer required, including upon termination of employment or change of role.
NIS2-RM-09c.manual_steps: Automated checks verify that unused access keys are identified (>45 days), old keys are flagged for rotation (>90 days), users with multiple active keys are detected, and IAM Access Analyzer identifies external access. Manual review required: access review schedule, evidence of periodic recertification, offboarding checklists, and timely revocation of access upon termination.
NIS2-RM-09c.evidence_template: Access review and deprovisioning controls verified: unused access keys identified (>45 days), key rotation flagged (>90 days), multiple active keys detected, IAM Access Analyzer for external access review. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary} Manual review of access review schedule and deprovisioning procedures is also required.

NIS2-RM-10.title: Use of multi-factor authentication, secured communications, and secured emergency communication systems
NIS2-RM-10.description: Essential and important entities shall use multi-factor authentication or continuous authentication solutions, secured voice, video and text communications, and secured emergency communication systems within the entity, where appropriate.
NIS2-RM-10.manual_steps: Automated checks verify MFA enforcement: root account MFA, user MFA, and hardware MFA for root. Manual review required: MFA policy covering all access points, secured communication channels (encrypted email, messaging, video conferencing), and emergency communication procedures.
NIS2-RM-10.evidence_template: Multi-factor authentication and secure communications verified: root account MFA, user MFA enforcement, hardware MFA for root. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary} Manual review of secured communications and emergency communication systems is also required per Article 21(2)(j).

NIS2-RM-10a.title: Multi-factor authentication
NIS2-RM-10a.description: The entity shall implement multi-factor authentication or continuous authentication solutions for access to network and information systems, particularly for remote access, privileged access, and access to sensitive data.
NIS2-RM-10a.manual_steps: Verify: root account MFA enabled, all IAM users have MFA enabled, hardware MFA for root account, CloudWatch alarm for console sign-in without MFA. Review MFA policy, approved MFA methods, and enforcement mechanisms for all access points.
NIS2-RM-10a.evidence_template: Multi-factor authentication verified: root account MFA, all IAM users MFA, hardware MFA for root, alarm for console sign-in without MFA. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

NIS2-RM-10b.title: Secure communication channels
NIS2-RM-10b.description: The entity shall use secured voice, video, and text communication solutions, and ensure that emergency communication systems are available and secured, including out-of-band communication channels for use during incidents.
NIS2-RM-10b.manual_steps: Automated checks verify that data channels enforce encryption (S3 HTTPS enforcement) and that network traffic is logged (VPC flow logs). Manual review required: secured communication tools inventory (encrypted email, messaging, video), emergency communication plan with out-of-band channels, and communication security testing.
NIS2-RM-10b.evidence_template: Secure communication controls verified: S3 HTTPS enforcement for data channels, VPC flow logs for network traffic visibility. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary} Manual review of secured voice, video, and text communication solutions and emergency communication systems is also required.

NIS2-IR-01.title: 24-hour early warning to CSIRT
NIS2-IR-01.description: In the case of a significant incident, the entity shall submit an early warning to the CSIRT or competent authority without undue delay and in any event within 24 hours of becoming aware of the significant incident. The early warning shall indicate whether the incident is suspected of being caused by unlawful or malicious acts, and whether it could have a cross-border impact.
NIS2-IR-01.manual_steps: Automated checks verify detection capabilities that support timely awareness: GuardDuty enabled and findings addressed, CloudWatch alarms for root usage and unauthorized API calls, security contact registered for AWS notifications. Manual review required: 24-hour early warning procedure, CSIRT contact information, notification template, escalation triggers for significant incidents, and evidence of process testing.
NIS2-IR-01.evidence_template: Detection infrastructure supporting 24h early warning verified: GuardDuty enabled and findings addressed, root usage and unauthorized API call alarms configured, security contact registered. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary} Manual review of 24-hour early warning procedure and CSIRT notification process is required per Article 23(4)(a).

NIS2-IR-02.title: 72-hour incident notification
NIS2-IR-02.description: Without undue delay and in any event within 72 hours of becoming aware of a significant incident, the entity shall submit an incident notification to the CSIRT or competent authority. The notification shall update the early warning and provide an initial assessment of the incident, including its severity and impact, and indicators of compromise where available.
NIS2-IR-02.manual_steps: Automated checks verify audit trail and forensic evidence capabilities: CloudTrail enabled with log validation, S3 object-level write and read logging for evidence preservation, VPC flow logs for network forensics. Manual review required: 72-hour notification procedure, incident assessment methodology, notification template with severity and impact fields, and IoC collection process.
NIS2-IR-02.evidence_template: Forensic and audit trail infrastructure supporting 72h notification verified: CloudTrail enabled with log validation, S3 object-level write and read logging, VPC flow logs enabled. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary} Manual review of 72-hour notification procedure and incident assessment methodology is required per Article 23(4)(b).

NIS2-IR-03.title: Final report within one month
NIS2-IR-03.description: Not later than one month after the submission of the incident notification, the entity shall submit a final report to the CSIRT or competent authority containing a detailed description of the incident, the type of threat or root cause, applied and ongoing mitigation measures, and the cross-border impact of the incident where applicable.
NIS2-IR-03.manual_steps: Automated checks verify the integrity of the full audit trail required for root cause analysis: CloudTrail enabled with log validation, CloudTrail bucket not public, CloudTrail bucket access logging, CloudTrail KMS encryption, S3 object-level write and read logging, S3 access logging. Manual review required: final report template, root cause analysis methodology, lessons-learned process, evidence preservation procedures, and cross-border impact assessment.
NIS2-IR-03.evidence_template: Audit trail integrity for final report preparation verified: CloudTrail enabled with log validation, bucket not public, bucket access logging, KMS encryption, S3 object-level logging, S3 access logging. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary} Manual review of final report procedures and root cause analysis methodology is required per Article 23(4)(d).

NIS2-GOV-01.title: Proportionate measures based on risk exposure
NIS2-GOV-01.description: Under Article 21(1), cybersecurity risk-management measures shall be proportionate to the risks posed, taking into account the state of the art, relevant European and international standards, the cost of implementation, and the entity's size, risk exposure, and the likelihood and severity of incidents. Article 21(3) requires Member States to ensure measures are proportionate, taking into account the degree of the entity's exposure to risks, its size, and the likelihood and severity of potential incidents including societal and economic impact.
NIS2-GOV-01.manual_steps: Automated checks verify that foundational risk identification services are enabled as a proportionate baseline. Manual review required: documented risk-based justification for the chosen level of security measures, proportionality assessment considering entity size and risk exposure, reference to applicable European and international standards (e.g., ISO 27001, ENISA guidelines), and cost-benefit documentation.
NIS2-GOV-01.evidence_template: Foundational risk identification services as proportionate baseline verified: GuardDuty enabled, Security Hub enabled, IAM Access Analyzer active, AWS Config recording. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary} Manual review of proportionality assessment and risk-based justification for security measures is required per Article 21(1) and 21(3).

NIS2-GOV-02.title: Management body approval and oversight
NIS2-GOV-02.description: Under Article 20(1), Member States shall ensure that the management bodies of essential and important entities approve the cybersecurity risk-management measures adopted by those entities, oversee their implementation, and can be held liable for infringements. The management body has ultimate responsibility for the entity's cybersecurity posture.
NIS2-GOV-02.manual_steps: Review evidence that the management body has formally approved cybersecurity risk-management measures, minutes of management body meetings discussing cybersecurity, oversight mechanisms (dashboards, reports, KPIs), and documentation of management body accountability. Verify awareness of potential personal liability under Article 20(1) and applicable Member State transposition law.
NIS2-GOV-02.evidence_template: NIS2-GOV-02 requires manual assessment. Review management body approval of cybersecurity measures, oversight documentation, meeting minutes, and accountability mechanisms per Article 20(1).

NIS2-GOV-03.title: Management body cybersecurity training
NIS2-GOV-03.description: Under Article 20(2), Member States shall ensure that members of the management bodies of essential and important entities are required to follow training, and shall encourage essential and important entities to offer similar training to their employees on a regular basis, in order to gain sufficient knowledge and skills to identify risks and assess cybersecurity risk-management practices and their impact on the services provided by the entity.
NIS2-GOV-03.manual_steps: Review evidence that management body members have completed cybersecurity training, training content and curriculum, attendance records, training frequency, and whether the training covers risk identification and assessment of cybersecurity practices. Verify that the entity offers similar training to employees on a regular basis.
NIS2-GOV-03.evidence_template: NIS2-GOV-03 requires manual assessment. Review management body cybersecurity training records, training curriculum, attendance logs, and employee training program per Article 20(2).
